# afdx

# Diagnosis performance using attributable fraction

This R-package help on the estimation of diagnosis performance
(Sensitivity, Specificity, Positive predictive value, Negative predicted
value) of a diagnostic test where the golden standard can’t be measured
but can be estimated using the attributable fraction

Two methods are presented with examples for Malaria diagnosis, using a
maximum likelihood estimated logistic exponential model and using a
bayesian latent class model.

To install the package from github use:

`devtools::install_github("johnaponte/afdx", build_manual = T, build_vignettes = T)`

# Package index

## All functions

- [`afdx`](https://johnaponte.github.io/afdx/reference/afdx-package.md)
  [`afdx-package`](https://johnaponte.github.io/afdx/reference/afdx-package.md)
  : afdx: Diagnosis performance indicators from attributable fraction
  estimates.
- [`get_latent_model()`](https://johnaponte.github.io/afdx/reference/get_latent_model.md)
  : Template for the bayesian latent class model
- [`logitexp()`](https://johnaponte.github.io/afdx/reference/logitexp.md)
  : Exponential logit model for two variables
- [`make_cutoffs()`](https://johnaponte.github.io/afdx/reference/make_cutoffs.md)
  : Cut-off points for densities and fever
- [`make_n_cutoffs()`](https://johnaponte.github.io/afdx/reference/make_n_cutoffs.md)
  : Make a defined number of categories having similar number of
  positives in each category
- [`malaria_df1`](https://johnaponte.github.io/afdx/reference/malaria_df1.md)
  : Synthetic data simulating a malaria crossectional
- [`malaria_df2`](https://johnaponte.github.io/afdx/reference/malaria_df2.md)
  : Synthetic data simulating a malaria crossectional
- [`senspec()`](https://johnaponte.github.io/afdx/reference/senspec.md)
  : S3 methods to estimate diagnosis performance of an afmodel

### All vignettes

- [Attributable fraction using a logitexponetial
  model](https://johnaponte.github.io/afdx/articles/af_logit_exponential.md):
- [Attributable fraction using a latent class
  model](https://johnaponte.github.io/afdx/articles/latentclass.md):
